segment-text: (Based on the screenplay "Let's Make Friends by Larry Levin)
EXT. EMPTY LOT - NEAR DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES - DUSK

The lights of downtown L.A. twinkle in the distance. PETER KLAVEN, late 20s-early 30s, nice looking, approachable, stands in front of a fenced in lot with his attractive girlfriend, ZOOEY RICE, same age, a little artsier-looking.
So, my plan is to build a cluster
of live-work lofts around the
perimeter here, and then create
Really? Denise keeps talking about wanting to open another branch.

It'd be great. The land is a little pricey, so, I won't be able to develop it right away, but, when I sell the Ferrigno Estate, I figured out, I'll at least be able to put a down payment on it, and still have enough money left over
for the reception in Santa Barbara.
Peter, what are you talking about?
Beat. Peter pulls a small JEWELRY BOX from his blazer. look of realization washes over Zooey's face.
I know it's only been eight months, but, I am madly, insanely, ridiculously in love with you.
He opens the box, revealing a lovely DIAMOND ENGAGEMENT RING.
Will you marry me?
Off her look of complete, utter happiness...
INT. PETER'S CAR - NIGHT
Peter drives. Zooey is in the passenger seat, glowing. She speaks over the car's BLUETOOTH SPEAKERPHONE with one of her two best friends, HAILEY.
...it just happened like two minutes ago. Hailey, can you believe it?!
How have I been on like ten million dates and you end up marrying some totally awesome guy who randomly walks into our store?!
(quietly, to Peter) Doesn't realize she's on speaker... (into car microphone) Will you conference in Denise? Hold on.
HAILEY (O.S.)
Zooey grabs Peter's hand as she waits...
That piece of land is perfect.
I'm so excited you like it. The neighborhood is like, two years
away from taking off, but-DENISE, Zooey's other best friend, comes on the line.
I cannot fucking believe you didn't call me first.
You are such a freak! Hailey's before you in my speed dial!

I'm kidding. Zooey, oh my god! I am so psyched for you it is disgusting. Hold on, Barry goes crazy if I'm in the house during his poker night. (MORE) DENISE (O.S.) (CONT'D) (to her husband) Give me a second you fat douchebag. Zooey just got engaged. Peter. You've met him twenty times, dumdum. (into phone) Sorry, Zo. So, did you guys pick a date?
June 30th in Santa Barbara. Peter already booked that place Bacara where we went for that long weekend.
Oh, he is so sweet. Wait, is Bacara the one where you guys slept together for the first time?
No, wasn't that in the hot tub in Mexico?
That's right. It was Mexico.
You told them about the hot tub? Maybe.
I don't remember.
Wow, so June 30th?
I mean, it's soon, but-DENISE (O.S.) Who cares? Peter's a doll, and he goes down on you six nights a week. What the hell are you waiting for?
Peter looks at Zooey in disbelief.
You're right. It's gonna be great. I gotta go. I'll call you later. Love you.
Love you!
So fucking psyched for you!
How do they know so much about our sex life?
Hailey and Denise are my best friends in the world. Girls share stuff like that. As Peter considers this...
Ah! We're engaged! I'm so excited!
She kisses him passionately, as we go to...
EXT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Hand in hand, Zooey and Peter walk towards their cozy bungalow in the Silverlake section of L.A.. Zooey's on her cell-phone.
...I know, he's the best. I feel so lucky. (quietly, to Peter) It's Debbie. She's freaking out. (back to phone) Alright, I gotta call Lisa, call me later, okay? Love you, bye.
They enter the house.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - NIGHT
It's decorated in a minimalist way with cool, eclectic furniture and knickknacks. Zooey is on the phone with another friend.
...love you too, Lisa. tomorrow.
She hangs up.
Turns to Peter.
I'm sorry, sweetie, I'm totally hogging the phone.
No, that's fine. I just want to give my parents a call.
THE LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
Peter speaks with his mom, JOYCE, on speakerphone.
...Mom, what do you think she said?! She said yes!
Oh, honey, I'm so thrilled for you. And you did it in front of that piece of land like we talked about?
Yes, she loved it. Thanks, that was such a good idea.
I didn't know that was her idea. Thank you, Joyce!
In the b.g., the sound of MEN EXULTING.
Dad sounds really excited. told him?
Actually, he and Robbie are watching some kind of boxing program.
Peter's father, OSWALD "OZ" KLAVEN shouts out...
Ultimate Fighting!
Do they want to get on the phone?
I'm sure. Hold on, honey. (after a moment) Apparently, they're in the middle of the game, sweetheart. But they say congratulations.
Oh, okay. No prob. Alright, well, we'll talk to you tomorrow.
Bye, Joyce!
Kids... I'm thrilled. Goodnight.
She hangs up.
Was that weird that your dad and brother didn't want to pick up?
No, I'm sure they'll call back. Wow, I am exhausted. Getting engaged is intense. You want to go up to bed?
It's only nine. You don't want to call any of your friends?
Nah, I'll make some calls tomorrow.
Really? You don't want to call that guy Tevin? Don't you speak like twenty times a day?
Yeah, but, he's more like a coworker than a friend.
Or Gil, from your fencing club?
He's not really a 'Call right away to tell him the news' kind of friend.
So, who do you consider your closest friends?
Uh, you know there's a bunch of guys. Samir Patel, my freshman roommate from UCLA.
I've never heard you mention him.
No? One Beer Samir? We're pretty tight. You're right, I should give him a ring.
THE LIVING ROOM - MINUTES LATER
Peter's on the phone. Zooey putters around in the b.g., pretending not to be paying attention.

Hey, Samir? It's Peter... Peter Klaven. Yeah, man, I guess it's been a little while. You been good? Great, well, I just wanted you to know that I got engaged tonight, so, we're getting married in three months and... Samir, no, I didn't bail on your wedding, I just, I couldn't make it. I thought I sent a gift though... no? Oh, well... I'm sorry, man. I didn't know you were so... okay, right, you take care as well.
He hangs up. Looks at Zooey.
When's the last time you spoke to him?
I guess it was a couple years ago.
Why didn't you go to his wedding?
I wanted to, but, I promised Lindsay I'd go to her family reunion that weekend.
And he's your closest friend?
He's... no, I have a bunch of good friends. I have the guys from my fencing club. There's Bob Loden.
Your chiropractor?

He's a great guy. Honestly, I think Samir has a drinking problem. He must've forgotten how close we were. (off her skeptical look) Don't look at me like that. I have friends. Just 'cause they don't call me every two seconds like Hailey and Denise, doesn't mean they don't exist.
EXT. PETER'S PARENTS' (THE KLAVEN'S) HOUSE - NIGHT
Peter and Zooey stand at the front door. JOYCE KLAVEN, 50s, hugs Peter. Then Zooey. Ad libs her congratulations.
Where's Dad?
In the den. Robbie gave him this Nintendo Wee or Wah, who knows what, for Father's Day. They're obsessed. (shouting) Boys, Peter and Zooey are here!
Out in a minute! I'm about to kick your son's ass in Tiger Woods!
INT. THE KLAVEN'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Peter and Zooey sit around the dining room table with Joyce, Peter's soft-spoken but macho, guy's-guy father, OZ, as well as his cute, gay younger brother, ROBBIE.
...did Peter have any good friends growing up?
She looks to Oz, who shakes his head.
I honestly don't seem to remember any.
Zooey, just to clarify here, Dad worked for IBM. We moved every eighteen months.
Robbie always managed to have friends. I mean, he probably wanted to suck their dicks, but-Oswald!
Mom, he's actually kind of got a point.
Well, he still doesn't need to use that language. The point is, Zooey, Peter always connected with women.
I can see that. He's a great boyfriend.
Also, you need to remember that he matured sexually at a very young age. I remember taking him swimming when he was twelve, the kid had a bush like a forty year old Serbian.
Dad, please stop speaking.
No, but, you always had a girlfriend, as far back as I can remember.
He wasn't a guy's guy. Your brother here is into video games, fishing, we golf together...
What are you talking about? sports.
You prance around in tights with a sword. It's not a sport. Oh, snap!
I'm a competitive fencer. the Olympic Games!
It's in
Oz, you two didn't do father-son stuff together when Peter was a kid?
Almost never. He spent all his time hanging out with his mother or circling real estate listings.
I was nurturing an interest that became my career.
A woman's career.
There's plenty of male real estate agents.
It's just something of a faggoty job, that's all.
"Faggoty?!" You can't use that word!
Yes, I can. Robbie gave me permission.
Dad loves the gays. I made him an honorary homo last week.
Guys, this is ridiculous. Why is it weird that I had girlfriends?
We're just saying you never had a best friend. That's all.
Who's your best friend?

I have two. Hank Mardukas has been my closest friend in the world since our first year at IBM, he was Best Man at my wedding, we speak three times a week for the past thirty years. And then, there's Robbie.
Robbie is your other best friend? Correct.
And Hank Mardukas.

Zooey, here's the deal. Peter's a girlfriend guy. Okay? Girlfriend guy's never have good male friends because they put all their energy into their girlfriends so their male friendships fall by the wayside.
Zooey, don't listen to him. barely knows me.
Peter, I happen to be an expert on men, and I know it's true. We are who we are.

Well put, son. We are who we are. Look, you think I was happy when Robbie told us he was a flamer? Of course not, but, I got used to the idea that he craved a wiener in his caboose after about five minutes, and the truth is he's cool as hell.
Dad, I actually prefer to be the top.
I know you do, pal, I'm speaking metaphorically here.
Alright, that's enough. let's clear the table. Come on,
Peter stands up to help his mom. See?
I can't help Mom with the dishes?
Do you see Robbie and me lifting a finger? (points to him and Robbie) Guy's guys.
Peter looks very unhappy.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
The newly engaged couple get ready for bed.
You know what? I completely forgot. I do have a best friend.
Who's that, sweetie? You.
Peter, I'm your girlfriend-Fiance.
Fiance, whatever. I don't want to be your best friend. (MORE)
Don't get me wrong... I love you. It's just different.
You don't feel like you can be open and honest with me? I thought we talk about everything.
We talk about almost everything. There's just certain things I talk about with Hailey and Denise that I could never... it's just different, that's all.
Well, I guess I don't have any close male friends. So, do you not want to marry me anymore?
Of course not. I really don't care. I just want you to be happy, that's all.
I'm very happy. It's not like this
is something I've been missing. Peter takes a ZANTAC from a bottle on his night-table Swallows it with some water.
What's wrong? Your acid reflux?
A little bit. It's fine.
They get into bed.
I'm just thinking, for the ceremony, I want a bunch of Bridesmaids up there, plus my Maid of Honor... Right...
But if I have six girls standing with me, and you don't have anyone, it might seem a bit uneven, no? (MORE)
Are you thinking Robbie might be your Best Man?
Robbie? No. Like I said, we're really not close.
You know what, don't worry about it. We'll figure something out. (then) Oooh, it's my turn to host Ladies Night tomorrow.
No prob. I have fencing practice. I'll go for a drink with the guys afterwards. (realizing) I'm telling you, those dudes are my friends.
I know, baby.
They start kissing. Things are heating up, when... the phone rings. Zooey turns on her bedside light, picks up the phone, eyeing the Caller I.D..
It's Hailey. She had a blind date. I'll be two seconds. I just want
to make sure she's okay.
INT./EXT. PETER'S CAR - MORNING

As Peter drives to work, through the car windows, he takes in a world of MALE FRIENDSHIP he never noticed before. -He sees a group of CONSTRUCTION WORKERS laughing. -FOUR SUIT-WEARING GUYS gabbing away as they carpool. -Two JOCKS jogging together. -A BILLBOARD with two SWEATY BUDDIES, post-workout chugging an ENERGY DRINK. -A cluster of OLD RUSSIAN MEN playing CHESS and KIBITZING at an outdoor cafe. EXT. REMAX REALTY OFFICE - DAY - ESTABLISHING
INT. REMAX REALTY OFFICE - DAY
A typical real estate office. Filled primarily with WOMEN.
IN THE KITCHEN AREA
Peter fixes a coffee as he speaks to a few FEMALE CO-WORKERS.
...she was completely surprised.
FEMALE CO-WORKER #1 Oh, Peter, that's so romantic. FEMALE CO-WORKER #2
for you.
Ooh, I'm so jealous. You're gonna be the best husband!
MAIN OFFICE AREA - MOMENTS LATER

As Peter heads towards his desk, he passes the cubicle of a handsome, cheesy guy named TEVIN DOWNEY -- perma-tan, blonde streaks in his hair. He is cracking up from something on his computer when he looks up.
What's so funny?
One of the guys in my fantasy football league sent me a Quicktime of a grandma riding a Sybian machine.
I don't know what that is?
It's a vibrating saddle that women sit on to give them super-intense orgasms. Check this out.
Peter looks at the monitor. And winces.
How sick is that? Very.
That is very sick.
So, what up, Dog? How's the Ferrigno dealio coming?
Coming good. Getting ready for the Open House in a couple weeks.
It's a big piece of house, so, if you want a co-pilot on this one, let me know, okay? Be happy to team up, help you "Git R Done."
What does that mean, "Git R Done?"
Larry the Cable Guy? comedian?
Oh, yeah, I don't really know his stuff. Anyway, I think I got it under control, but thanks for the
Tevin goes back to his computer. Peter takes a final glance at the sick image on the screen, walks away.
INT. FENCING GYMNASIUM - NIGHT
Two MEN, dressed in fencing gear, expertly parry with each other. They finish, remove their masks, revealing that one of them is Peter. They salute each other, as fencers do.
INT. FENCING GYMNASIUM - LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
A post-shower Peter and some other FENCERS get dressed.
Nice job stealing my tempo there, Peter.
I set you up for a solid attack on the blade, but you parried back pretty well.
Anybody seen my Manchette?
Under your Plastron, dickweed.
They jostle with each other -- the way men do in a locker room. Another fencer, GIL, talks to Peter.
I told my cousin the open house is a week from Saturday, right?
Yeah, it's gonna be great. I appreciate you hooking me up with him, Gil. It's a really big deal for me.
You sold me my house, right? You're the most honest real estate agent I know.
Thanks. And look, I know you're the middle-man here, but, if your cousin has any questions, he should feel free to call me.
I will let him know.
Great. So, you guys want to get some grub? Or, grab a beer or something?
The fencers exchange uncomfortable looks.
We're actually, uh, we're driving up to Joshua Tree tonight. Oh, yeah? there?
What's going on up
(jostles Fencer #1) We're taking Eugene on a little bachelor party slash camping trip.
I didn't know you're getting married.
I am, indeed. Next Sunday.
Great. Cool deal. You doing kind of a small thing, or-EUGENE Um, it's like three... twenty five. I managed to rope these boys into being my groomsmen.
Very, very cool.
Awkward beat. Then...
I would've invited you, but, you never really come out with us after practice. I didn't think you-PETER Dude, don't sweat it. Seriously, no worries. It's funny, I just got engaged myself.
That's great, Peter. Your fiance designs book jackets, right?
No, that was Heather, she was a couple of girlfriends ago. Zooey owns a home furnishing store in Silverlake.
Awesome, man. Good luck with that.
Alright, we have a long drive. We should hit the road. Later, Peter.
Have fun, boys.
Peter waves, watches them exit -- joking with each other. sits there alone in the locker room, sadly packing up. He
EXT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Peter is about to enter through the front door. Through the window, he sees Zooey and her GIRLFRIENDS sitting around the living room, drinking wine, laughing. He heads to the back of the house.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - BACK DOOR - NIGHT
Peter quietly lets himself in.
INT. PETER'S HOME OFFICE - NIGHT

Peter is on his computer. ON THE SCREEN -- he works on the online listing for a spectacular, MODERN HOUSE in the Hollywood Hills. He fiddles with the layout and description. The sound of female laughter is audible O.S.. INT.
PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT

Peter prepares a tray of ROOT BEER FLOATS for the ladies. As he does, he looks out the kitchen window, sees his MALE NEIGHBOR in his house across the way -- watching a BASKETBALL GAME with his FRIENDS. They all react as Kobe dunks.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
Zooey hangs out with a circle of LADIES, including HAILEY, single and miserable about it, and DENISE, caustic and married.
He didn't make any friends at UCLA?
He lived alone after freshman year.
Ooh. The ones who lived alone we're always the biggest freakazoids.
Peter's not a freakazoid. He had a serious girlfriend all through college.
Look, we're not saying he's a freakazoid, but the truth is, for the future of your marriage, it's a little troubling. If Barry didn't have any friends I'd shoot myself in the face.
The ladies laugh. BY THE DOOR TO THE KITCHEN, Peter is about to interrupt when he just stops... and LISTENS.

I'm serious. You know what my favorite nights are? Hanging out with you guys. And I can do it guilt free because Barry loves hanging out with his friends too. Every month there's a golf weekend, a ski-trip, a weekend in Vegas.
You let him go to Vegas? Aren't you worried he's cheating on you?
He's forty pounds overweight with a Jew-fro and a small dick. (off their looks/laughter) I love the guy to death, but I'm far and away the best looking woman he's gonna get, and I'm just a seven.
Come on! You are so not a seven! You're hot!
I know, I totally think I'm higher than a seven! I was testing you bitches!
So, who's going to be his Best Man?
I have no idea. I really think his Mom might be his closest friend.
The ladies react to/shudder at the thought of this.
This is a serious issue, Zo. Guys without friends can be superclingy.
My dad had no friends. It was awful. He dragged my mom to all these football games. She hated it. That's a big reason why they got divorced.
Look, the truth is, I'm freaking out, you guys! I know, I'm horrible for feeling this, but, I can't believe I'm marrying someone who has no friends!
Peter motions to turn back around when he makes a noise. Peter?
ZOOEY (CONT'D)
Peter opens the swinging door between the kitchen and the rest of the house... holding the tray of root beer floats.
Oh, hey, I just made you guys some Root Beer Floats.
The ladies greet Peter, ad lib -- thanking him, congratulating him on the engagement. Then...
I thought you were going out with your fencing team?
Yeah, they couldn't make it, so, I just came back here. Don't worry, I've been in my office working. I'm not even here. Bye, ladies.
He sets down the floats. Starts to exit.
Thanks for the floats, baby. was really sweet.
He waves, disappears.
You think he heard us?
Definitely not.
The ladies all shake their heads, trying to convince themselves.
INT. PETER'S HOME OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
He stares out the window, watching his neighbor say goodbye to his friends -- giving half-hugs, cool handshakes, etc.
EXT. EQUINOX GYM - WEST HOLLYWOOD - DAY
Buff MEN and WOMEN enter and exit this sleek gym.
INT. EQUINOX GYM - CONTINUOUS
Robbie Klaven wears a tight fitting tee-shirt that reads: TRAINER. He walks with a concerned looking Peter.
You need to find a Best Man for your wedding?! What are you, in a John Hughes movie?
I know, it sounds ridiculous, but, Zooey is really upset about this friend thing. You said at dinner you know all about men. I don't know where else to turn.
This is so exciting, you coming to me for help. The good news is, not only do I know about men, but straight guys are my specialty.
What does that mean?
I get bored pursuing gay guys. When you're cute and fit, you can pretty much have any fag you want. (MORE)
I like to set my sights on straight guys to give myself more of a challenge.
Does it work?
Peter, I get more butt-pussy from straight guys than you could ever imagine.
Just... please don't ever use that term with me again.
Robbie walks up to a bench press where a GUY is struggling with a bar-bell. Robbie adopts more of a straight-guy voice.
Come on! Push it out! It's all you! I'm not even helping! Little further! There you go!
The guy looks up at Robbie appreciatively.
Thanks, man.
No worries. I'll see you around. I hope so.
BENCH PRESS GUY
Robbie and the guy exchange smiles.
That guy was totally flirting with
I know, and did you see the wedding ring? Straight as an arrow. I'm telling you, hooking up is easy. Meeting platonic male friends, not so much.
Why is that?
Several reasons. First off, at your age, most men are settled into their groups of friends. Either they have friends from high school, college, or work. As time goes on,
the straight ones get married,
Alright, so, what do I do?

We have to put the word out on the street that you're available. I'll do some recon around the gym, but you need to be aggressive about this. Every man is a potential suitor. You see a guy on the street, strike up a conversation. Ask him on a man-date. By that, I mean a casual lunch or after-work drink. No dinner and no movies. You're not taking these guys to see THE DEVIL WEARS PRADA, understand? Yes.
Keep it casual.
Men are very sensitive about meeting new friends. Why? Because they're all secretly afraid that they're gay, and half of them are. So, they overcompensate by being
emotionally distant and guarded. An incredibly STRAIGHT-SEEMING GUY walks by, exchanges "Hey's" with Robbie.
Let me guess, you slept with that guy?
No, but he gave me a handjob in the steam room last week. It was terrible, 'cause his fingers were all calloused from weightlifting.
See, this is why we don't hang out. You're disgusting.
Robbie shrugs, unfazed.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Peter and Zooey sit on the living room couch watching the last scene of BRIDGET JONES'S DIARY.
How is it that Renee Zellwegger is
I know, it's crazy. Oh my god,
Colin Firth's sweater-PETER
Way to put this on your Netflix cue, baby.
Peter walks to the TV, ejects the DVD.
So, I know this might sound weird, but I'm going to try to meet
someone before the wedding.
What are you talking about?
A friend. So, if you, or any of your zillion girlfriends have any candidates, I just want you to know, I'm available.
You're so ridiculous. You're going to try and meet a friend?
Why not? I want a Best Man up there with me. I don't want to look like a big loser when you have twenty eight bridesmaids, and I can't even get One Beer Samir to show up on my end.
I know you're just doing this for me.
Yeah, but, I really love you, so, it kind of feels good.
I am so happy you walked into our store that day.
She kisses him.
You wanna try and do it before
Hailey gets home from her new date?
Shut up, jerk. (then) She'll be back in a few minutes, so it's gonna have to be a quickie.
And as they start taking off their clothes, we FADE UP A SONG and begin a montage of Peter's man-dates. We see the setups, and the dates in a series of SPLIT-SCREENS AND JUMPCUTS.
INT. BARRY AND DENISE'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Denise talks quietly to her husband, BARRY, in the doorway. Behind them, Peter sits at a poker table with BARRY'S ASSHOLE FRIENDS.
The guy has no idea how to play poker!
Barry, this is important to Zooey, so shut the fuck up and be nice.
Only if we can have sex with the lights on when you get home. Fine.
Love you too.
INT. REMAX REALTY OFFICE - DAY
Peter is talking to his female co-workers...
...so if you know any available men, just feel free to give 'em my number, or E-mail if that's easier.
OLDER FEMALE CO-WORKER
EXT. UPSCALE RESTAURANT - NIGHT
As Peter valets his car, we hear his MOM talking in V.O..
Honey, he just moved to L.A.. Barely knows anyone. He's an architect. His mother says he's so excited to meet you.
Peter waves to an attractive guy, DOUG, waiting outside. guy mouths, 'Peter?' Peter nods, they shake hands.
INT. EQUINOX GYM - WEST HOLLYWOOD - DAY
Robbie talks to a CLIENT as he helps him with a lat pulldown.
...my brother's a great guy, fun to be around. (beat) (MORE)
And I'll give you five free sessions if you go out with him.
INT. STAPLES CENTER - NIGHT
Peter watches a basketball game with Robbie's gym client, who we now realize has A VERY HIGH VOICE.
Pass the goddamn rock, you piece of shit! He's so selfish, you know?
He really is.
What is your problem, Zebra?! was a foul!
Peter is clearly embarrassed to be with the guy.
INT. JANS FAMILY RESTAURANT - DAY
Peter eats an Early Bird Special with an OLD MAN who studies the chocolate drink in front of him. OLD MAN The perfect Egg Cream is two parts seltzer water, one part whole milk,
one part U-Bet Chocolate Syrup. No
Peter nods, trying to hide his boredom.
INT. BARRY AND DENISE'S HOUSE - NIGHT
The poker game is in progress. the middle.
I guess I'm all in. you say it?
Peter pushes his CHIPS into Is that how
Yes, Peter, that is how you say it.
I'm sure you beat me. I just have five of the same suit or whatever.
Barry looks like he's ready to kill Peter.
INT. UPSCALE RESTAURANT - NIGHT
Peter and Doug, the attractive guy, are hitting it off as they share a bottle of wine, getting a little tipsy.
INT. PETER AND ZOOEY'S HOUSE - HOME OFFICE - NIGHT

As Peter works on the website for the Ferrigno Estate, he looks out his window, sees his neighbor now hosting a backyard barbecue with his buddies. Peter GOOGLES: 'How do you meet straight male friends?'. His eyes land on a site called "MEET-NEW-FRIENDS.COM."
INT. STAPLES CENTER - NIGHT
The high-voiced guy is chanting at the top of his lungs. HIGH VOICED GUY LET'S GO... LAKERS! LET'S GO LAKERS! Peter winces, covers his ears in agony.
INT. JANS FAMILY RESTAURANT - DAY
The old man has FALLEN ASLEEP at the table. wipes DROOL from his mouth with his napkin. Peter gently
INT. BARRY AND DENISE'S HOUSE - NIGHT

Barry and his friends play a drinking game called BOAT RACES - where you must chug a beer then tap the guy next to you who chugs as well, until one team finishes first. On his team, Peter gets tapped, starts CHUGGING his beer, until he gags and THROWS UP all over the GUY next to him.
EXT. UPSCALE RESTAURANT - NIGHT
Peter and Doug exit, hand their tickets to the VALET.
I gotta tell you, I really enjoyed this, Doug. (MORE)
I've been out with so many jerks, it's great to finally meet someone I can have an actual conversation with.
Let's do it again.
I'd love to.
Tomorrow night? o'clock?
The Valet pulls up in Doug's car.
I will see you there, sir.
Peter starts to extend his hand, when Doug reaches out AND KISSES PETER ON THE MOUTH. He withdraws, enters his car, leaving Peter standing there, stunned.
INT. PETER'S CAR - MOMENTS LATER
Peter is on speakerphone with his mom.
He put his tongue in my goddamn mouth, Mom!
How could I know he was gay?! All Rita said was her son wanted to meet my son!
And you didn't think for a minute that she was talking about your son who sleeps with men?!
